title: atriplex pusilla
intro: Atriplex pusilla, also known as sand-bur, is a type of succulent shrub that belongs to the amaranth family and is native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Atriplex pusilla include treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations, as well as being used as a diuretic and to reduce fever. Herbal preparations of Atriplex pusilla include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
